new_articles.title: The Walls of Jericho
new_articles.subtitle: Did They Really Fall?
new_articles.content:
## The Ancient City

Jericho is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities on Earth. The Bible describes its walls collapsing after the Israelites marched around it for seven days.

## What Archaeologists Found

Excavations led by Kathleen Kenyon in the 1950s confirmed that Jericho's walls fell outward — not inward as would happen in a normal siege. The collapsed mudbrick formed a ramp that attackers could walk right up.

## Grain Jars Found Full

One of the most remarkable discoveries was large storage jars still full of grain. This means the city was not looted before destruction — consistent with Joshua 6:18 where Israel was forbidden from taking plunder.

## The Date Debate

The main scholarly debate is about *when* these walls fell. Kenyon dated them to around 1550 BC. Bryant Wood re-examined the same evidence and dated it to around 1400 BC — matching the biblical timeline.

## Bottom Line

The walls of Jericho did fall. The archaeology matches Scripture's description in striking detail: walls that collapsed outward, a city that was not looted, ash layers showing fire. The only debate is the date — and that debate is ongoing.

new_articles.title: Was Paul a Reliable Witness?
new_articles.subtitle: Testing Paul's Credibility as a Historical Source
new_articles.primaryTags: paul, apostle, acts, credibility, history, new testament
new_articles.content:
## The Question

Skeptics often dismiss Paul as biased or unreliable. But does history support this dismissal?

## Paul's Credentials as a Witness

**1. Early source.** Paul's letters (especially 1 Corinthians and Galatians) date to within 20 years of Jesus's crucifixion — before any of the Gospels were written.

**2. He met eyewitnesses.** Galatians 1:18-19 records Paul spending 15 days with Peter and meeting James, the brother of Jesus — primary eyewitnesses.

**3. He had nothing to gain.** Paul (formerly Saul) was a persecutor of Christians. His conversion cost him his career, status, and eventually his life.

**4. The resurrection creed.** In 1 Corinthians 15:3-8, Paul passes on a creed that scholars date to within 2-5 years of the crucifixion — listing over 500 eyewitnesses.

## The Bottom Line

By any standard historical criteria, Paul is one of the most remarkable witnesses in ancient history. He sacrificed everything for what he claimed to have seen and experienced. His testimony deserves serious engagement.

daily_question.title: Excavations at Jericho found storage jars still full of grain after the city was destroyed. Why does this single detail powerfully confirm the biblical account?
options: It proves the city was wealthy and well-supplied
It matches Joshua 6:18 — Israel was forbidden from looting
It shows the attack happened during harvest season
It confirms the Israelites camped nearby for 40 years
daily_question.correct_answer: It matches Joshua 6:18 — Israel was forbidden from looting
daily_question.explanation: When cities were conquered in the ancient Near East, they were looted. Finding full grain jars means this city was NOT looted — which is highly unusual. Joshua 6:18 explains exactly why: God commanded Israel not to take any plunder from Jericho. The same archaeological layer also shows the walls collapsed outward (not inward as in a normal siege) and widespread ash from fire — matching Joshua 6:24. Every unusual detail in the archaeology matches an unusual detail in Scripture.
